text: Scientists have discovered that a common gut bacteria called Lactobacillus reuteri can significantly reduce anxiety and depression-like behaviors in mice. The bacteria appears to work by producing a compound that activates the vagus nerve, which connects the gut to the brain. When researchers removed this bacteria from the guts of the mice, their anxiety levels went up noticeably. When they restored it, the anxiety went back down. The team thinks this could eventually lead to new treatments for mental health conditions in humans, though they caution that mouse studies don't always translate directly to people. The findings were published in the journal Cell.

Our company had a really rough third quarter. Revenue dropped by 18 percent compared to the same period last year, which was mostly because of supply chain problems that made it hard to get products to customers on time. We also lost two big clients who went with cheaper competitors. The good news is that we have fixed most of the supply chain issues now and signed three new contracts worth a combined 4 million dollars. Management thinks we will be back to normal growth by Q1 next year if things keep going the way they are.

If you want to sleep better, one of the best things you can do is stop looking at your phone before bed. The blue light from screens messes with your melatonin levels and tricks your brain into thinking it is still daytime. Most sleep experts say you should put your phone away at least an hour before you want to go to sleep. Instead, try reading a physical book, doing some light stretching, or just sitting quietly. A lot of people find that after a week of doing this, they fall asleep faster and wake up feeling more rested.

The new software update introduces several breaking changes that developers need to be aware of before upgrading their production systems. The authentication module has been completely refactored and now requires OAuth 2.0 tokens instead of API keys. Legacy API key authentication will stop working after March 31st. Additionally, the rate limiting system has been updated: endpoints now allow a maximum of 100 requests per minute per user, down from 500. Any application that exceeds this limit will receive a 429 status code and must implement exponential backoff. Full migration documentation is available in the developer portal.

The Amazon rainforest is getting smaller every year. In 2023 alone, an area roughly the size of Belgium was cleared for farming and cattle ranching. This is a big problem because the Amazon produces about 20 percent of the world's oxygen and is home to more species of plants and animals than anywhere else on Earth. When the trees are cut down, all the carbon stored in them gets released into the atmosphere, making climate change worse. Some scientists are warning that if deforestation continues at this rate, large parts of the Amazon could turn into savanna within 30 years.
